key_insight: The paper presents a unified, SPI-controlled neuromorphic silicon suite that combines PVT sensing, stochastic LIF inference, STDP learning, and memristor-crossbar programming in one open standard-cell implementation.
engineering_relevance: It offers a compact, configurable hardware foundation for edge neuromorphic systems that need inference, learning, and analogue-memory interfacing on the same chip flow.
method: Built four interface-compatible digital IP blocks in SkyWater 130 nm standard-cell CMOS.
Implemented a PVT sensor with selectable ring oscillators, jitter-based TRNG, and frequency-health monitoring.
Added a stochastic LIF neuron, programmable STDP controller, and crossbar controller with forming/set/reset/read and I-V sweep support.
evidence_signal: The suite was validated with 99 passing cocotb tests at RTL and gate level, then synthesized and clocked to meet 50 MHz with positive timing margins and estimated 0.64–0.70 mW power per block.
watch_item: Results are based on simulation and the implementation flow only, so no fabricated silicon behavior or post-fabrication performance is yet demonstrated.
paper_title: Design and Development of a Neuromorphic Silicon Suite: PVT Sensing, Stochastic LIF Inference, On-Chip STDP Learning, and Crossbar Programming
